'''Toby King''' (born 9 July 1996)<ref name=profile /> is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|ce}} for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Betfred Super League]]. He has played for {{rlnt|Ireland}}, {{rlnt|England}} and the [[England Knights]] at international level.<ref name="England Knights v Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights v Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=24 October 2019}}</ref> 

He has spent time on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as well as season-long loans at both the [[Huddersfield Giants]] and the [[Wigan Warriors]] in the [[Super League]].

King made his {{rlnt|England}} debut on 29 April 2023 in the 64-0 victory over {{rlnt|France}} at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].

He will join the [[London Broncos]] ahead of the 2027 Super League season.<ref>[https://www.londonbroncosrl.com/toby-king-signs-with-london-broncos-from-2027 Toby King signs with London Broncos from 2027]</ref>

==Background==
King was born in [[Huddersfield]], [[West Yorkshire]], [[England]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.giantsrl.com/article/8131/king-joins-giants-in-loan-swap-deal |title=King joins Giants in loan swap deal|publisher=Huddersfield Giants|date=25 June 2022|access-date=12 October 2022}}</ref>

==Club career==
Toby is the younger brother of Adam and [[George King (rugby league)|George King]], and they played amateur rugby league together at Meltham All Blacks. They were spotted by the Wires’ Yorkshire scout Tommy Gleeson after both appeared for Huddersfield.

===Warrington===
King was promoted to [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] first team squad in 2014. King made his début in July 2014 during a 72–10 victory over [[London Broncos|London]], which was his only appearance in 2014. King next appeared in April 2015, scoring twice in an 80–0 rout against [[Wakefield Trinity]].<ref>{{cite web|last1=Gordon|first1=James|title=Brothers promoted to Wires' first-team squad|url=http://www.loverugbyleague.com/news_14580-brothers-promoted-to-wolves-first-team-squad.html|publisher=Love Rugby League|accessdate=23 July 2014|date=24 March 2014}}</ref>

King played in the [[2016 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by [[Hull F.C.]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Hull FC’s Jamie Shaul’s late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2016/aug/27/hull-warrington-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Hull FC's Jamie Shaul's late try takes Challenge Cup away from Warrington|publisher=Guardian|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref><ref name="Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/37169016|title=Challenge Cup final: Hull FC 12-10 Warrington Wolves|publisher=BBC|date=27 August 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>
King played in the [[2016 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2016/oct/08/super-league-grand-final-warrington-wigan-live|title=Warrington 6-12 Wigan: Super League Grand Final – as it happened!|publisher=Guardian|date=8 October 2016|accessdate=7 November 2019}}</ref>
King played in the [[2018 Challenge Cup Final]] defeat by the [[Catalans Dragons]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2018/aug/25/catalans-dragons-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final-match-report|title=Catalans Dragons beat Warrington in Challenge Cup final to make history|publisher=Guardian|date=25 August 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
King played in the [[2019 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley">{{cite web|url=https://www.mirror.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/st-helens-vs-warrington-live-18996678|title=St Helens 4-18 Warrington RESULT: Challenge Cup Final as it happened from Wembley|publisher=Mirror|date=24 August 2019|accessdate=1 November 2019}}</ref>
On 13 October 2018, King played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|accessdate=5 November 2019}}</ref>
On 7 June 2025, King played in Warrington's 8-6 Challenge Cup final loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/cn0gnqllpxlo|title=In pictures: Hull KR Challenge Cup victory parade|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants (loan)===
On 25 June 2022 it was announced that he would join the [[Huddersfield Giants]] for the remainder of the 2022 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/toby-king-jake-wardle-warrington-huddersfield-swap/|title=Warrington's Toby King and Huddersfield's Jake Wardle in swap deal|website=www.loverugbyleague.com|accessdate=27 Jun 2022}}</ref>

===Wigan Warriors (loan)===
In August 2022 it was announced that King had signed for the [[Wigan Warriors]] on a season-long loan deal.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/62652691|title=Wigan sign Toby King on loan for 2023 season|date=23 August 2022|website=BBC Sport}}</ref>
On 14 October 2023, King played in Wigan's [[2023 Super League Grand Final]] victory over the [[Catalans Dragons]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2023/oct/14/catalans-dragons-wigan-super-league-grand-final-rugby-league-match-report/|title=Wigan Warriors crowned Super League champions after downing Dragons|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>
On 8 June 2024, King played in Warrington's [[2024 Challenge Cup final]] defeat against Wigan.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c2jj1x7m71zo|title=Wigan beat Warrington to win Challenge Cup|website=www.bbc.co.uk}}</ref>

===London Broncos===
King will join the [[London Broncos]] ahead of the 2027 Super League season.<ref>[https://www.alloutrugbyleague.co.uk/news/london-broncos-boost-super-league-1871878 London Broncos boost Super League push with signing of Warrington Wolves star]</ref>

==International career==
In 2016 King was called up to the [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup qualification – Europe#Group B|2017 Rugby League World Cup European Pool B qualifiers]].<ref name="Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers">{{cite web|url=http://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/14763089.Warrington_Wolves_trio_named_in_Ireland_squad_for_World_Cup_qualifiers/|title=Warrington Wolves trio named in Ireland squad for World Cup qualifiers|publisher=Warrington Guardian|date=24 September 2016|accessdate=26 September 2016}}</ref>

In 2018 placed for [[England Knights]] on their tour of [[Papua New Guinea]], playing both matches  against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref name="Papua New Guinea vs England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718006#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea vs England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=27 October 2018|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref><ref name="Papua New Guinea v England Knights">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/papua-new-guinea-v-england-knights-international-2018/718007#lineup|title=Papua New Guinea v England Knights|publisher=WWOS|date=3 November 2018|accessdate=29 October 2019}}</ref> In 2019, he once again placed for England Knights against [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]].<ref name="England Knights vs Jamaica">{{cite web|url=https://wwos.nine.com.au/nrl/live-scores/england-knights-v-jamaica-international-2019/719012#lineup|title=England Knights vs Jamaica|publisher=WWOS|date=20 October 2019|accessdate=30 October 2019}}</ref>

King placed for Ireland in the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup|2021 World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|date=29 September 2022|title=Ireland's 24-man Rugby League World Cup 2021 squad|url=https://www.rli.ie/2022/09/29/ire-men-rlwc21-squad-announced/|website=rli.ie|access-date=29 September 2022}}</ref>
